question.text: Under the Liberalised Remittance Scheme (LRS), what is the maximum amount an individual can invest abroad every year?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Under RBI's Liberalised Remittance Scheme, an individual can invest up to $250,000 abroad every year. This route enables geographical and currency diversification and opens up international investment opportunities. **The Trap:** $100,000 was an older limit — the current limit is $250,000 per financial year. **Cross-Exam Context:** LRS limits are tested in X-A and relevant for currency derivatives (Series I) context. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** LRS can be used not just for investments but also for education, travel, and gifts — ensure clients understand the aggregate annual limit applies across all purposes.
